featured.title: Vercel confirms breach as hackers claim to be selling stolen data - BleepingComputer
featured.summary: Vercel confirms breach as hackers claim to be selling stolen data  BleepingComputer
featured.reason: Major data breach with potential data sale
featured.suggestedTitle: Vercel Data Breach: Stolen Data on Sale
featured.suggestedSummary: Vercel, a cloud development platform, has confirmed a security breach after threat actors claimed to have stolen and are now selling user data. Details on the extent of the data compromised are still emerging, but the breach highlights significant risks to user privacy and platform security. Security teams should monitor for any unusual account activities and consider implementing additional security measures to protect sensitive data.

featured.title: Carnival Corporation probes data breach after claims of 8.7M records theft - CyberInsider
featured.summary: Carnival Corporation probes data breach after claims of 8.7M records theft  CyberInsider
featured.suggestedSummary: Carnival Corporation is probing a data breach incident where attackers claim to have stolen 8.7 million records. The breach reportedly involves sensitive personal data, which could lead to identity theft and fraud. Security teams should be vigilant about monitoring for any signs of data misuse and ensure robust incident response plans are in place to mitigate potential impacts.

quickNews.title: Apple account change alerts abused to send phishing emails
quickNews.summary: Apple account change notifications are being abused to send fake iPhone purchase phishing scams within legitimate emails sent from Apple's servers, increasing legitimacy and potentially allowing them to bypass spam filters. [...]

quickNews.title: NIST to stop rating non-priority flaws due to volume increase
quickNews.summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology will stop assigning severity scores to lower-priority vulnerabilities due to the growing workload from rising submission volumes. [...]
